posts.name: Georgios Karathanasis
posts.text:
Dear all,
Firstly thanks for the excellent documentation. I was going through the parametric exercise and in part 5 I read this [*]. So I am a bit confused why in the fitting part we do setParameters pdfindex=i? Shouldnt be  setParameters pdfindex=j and freezeParameters pdfindex? Is it something technical that I am missing?
Best regards,
george
[*] You can then set which function is used for generating the toys with the --setParameters pdfindex=i option, and which function is used for fitting with --setParameters pdfindex=i --freezeParameters pdfindex=j options.
posts.cooked_html:
Dear all,

Firstly thanks for the excellent documentation. I was going through the parametric exercise and in part 5 I read this [*]. So I am a bit confused why in the fitting part we do setParameters pdfindex=i? Shouldnt be setParameters pdfindex=j and freezeParameters pdfindex? Is it something technical that I am missing?

Best regards,
george

[*] You can then set which function is used for generating the toys with the --setParameters pdfindex=i option, and which function is used for fitting with --setParameters pdfindex=i --freezeParameters pdfindex=j options.

posts.name: Kyle Cormier
posts.text:
Dead Georgios,
I think you’re right. If you try running with --freezeParameters  <name>=<value> you should get a warning like this:
WARNING: cannot freeze nuisance parameter pdfindex_Tag0=1 if it doesn't exist!

I think its just a mistake in the documentation. I’ve created a pull request to fix the documentation:

github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit

Fix arguments for setting pdfindex for toys in tutorial

cms-analysis:main ← kcormi:param_tutorial_docs_discrete_freeze_fix

opened 03:18PM - 29 Jun 23 UTC

kcormi

+1
-1

Fixing a small mistake in the parametric fit tutorial documentation about using …`RooMultiPDF`

As pointed out on cms talk, the current documentation says to use

```
--setParameters pdfindex=i --freezeParameters pdfindex=j
```
when doing the fit, to test the fit using a different pdf than was used to generate the toys. But the second part is not a valid argument, it should only take the parameter name, not a value.

thanks for pointing this out.
Best,
Kyle

I think its just a mistake in the documentation. I’ve created a pull request to fix the documentation:

opened 03:18PM - 29 Jun 23 UTC
kcormi
+1 -1

Fixing a small mistake in the parametric fit tutorial documentation about using …`RooMultiPDF` As pointed out on cms talk, the current documentation says to use ``` --setParameters pdfindex=i --freezeParameters pdfindex=j ``` when doing the fit, to test the fit using a different pdf than was used to generate the toys. But the second part is not a valid argument, it should only take the parameter name, not a value.

thanks for pointing this out.

Best,
Kyle
